components.symbol_description: Square box with diagonal line inside and tag label; likely a local panel or speed/signal conditioner
components.notes: Tag reads 'SPHN 0030'; square symbol with internal diagonal suggests local panel or junction box per ISA convention

components.symbol_description: Gate/ball valve symbol (two opposing triangles) on 2"x0.75" reduced line
components.notes: Located after 2"x0.75" reducer; part of utility connection (UC) line

components.symbol_description: Needle or globe valve symbol with dashed operator line (asterisk/star actuator symbol below)
components.notes: Dashed line with asterisk below valve suggests local manual or special operator; tag partially small

components.symbol_description: Check or globe valve (triangular arrow symbol) labeled NC (Normally Closed)
components.notes: NC annotation visible — Normally Closed; CO2 tag symbol visible adjacent indicating commissioning connection

components.symbol_description: Nozzle stub with flanged end connecting into vessel wall
components.notes: Connects horizontal utility line to vessel shell

components.symbol_description: Nozzle with flanged blind/spectacle plate, labeled NOTE-2; 12" stub below 16"x12" reducer
components.position_hint: upper right, second from right cluster
components.notes: NOTE-2 reference likely specifies blind flange or spectacle plate requirement; 12" nominal diameter

components.symbol_description: Nozzle with flanged blind/spectacle plate, labeled NOTE-2; 12" stub below 16"x12" reducer
components.notes: Identical configuration to N4; both reference NOTE-2

components.symbol_description: Nozzle at vessel top, partially cut off at right edge; 3" diameter indicated
components.position_hint: upper far right, cut off at right edge
components.notes: Only partially visible; tag and connection details continue into adjacent tile r2c2

components.symbol_description: Vessel side nozzle stub (2") connected to HV0024 valve
components.notes: 2" diameter; part of level gauge bridle or drain connection

components.symbol_description: Gate valve with XO symbol (cross with circle) indicating remotely operated or lock-open
components.notes: XO symbol typically denotes car-sealed open or normally open locked valve in P&ID convention

components.symbol_description: Circle instrument bubble labeled LG (Level Gauge / Level Glass)
components.notes: Local level glass/gauge; located between HV0024 and HV0025 suggesting bridle assembly

components.symbol_description: Vessel side nozzle stub (2") connected to HV0025 valve, below N18A
components.notes: Lower nozzle of level gauge bridle pair with N18A

components.symbol_description: Gate valve with XO symbol (cross with circle)
components.notes: Lower isolation valve of the LG-0001 bridle; mirrors HV0024 above

components.symbol_description: Gate or globe valve at right edge, partially visible
components.notes: Partially cut off; continuation expected in tile r2c2

components.symbol_description: Gate or globe valve at right edge, partially visible with IG symbol above
components.notes: Partially cut off at right edge; IG tag (instrument connection) visible adjacent

components.symbol_description: Small diamond or flag symbol labeled CO2; appears multiple times — likely commissioning connection markers
components.position_hint: multiple locations: center-right and right side
components.notes: CO2 repeated at several points; in P&ID context may denote chemical injection (CO2 inhibitor) or commissioning/blowdown tie-in; requires cross-reference with drawing legend

components.symbol_description: Text label 'UC' — Utility Connection terminus arrow
components.position_hint: center-left, on utility line leading to SPHN-0030
components.notes: UC = Utility Connection; indicates off-drawing tie-in point for utilities

components.symbol_description: Text label 'NC' — Normally Closed status tag
components.notes: Specifies normal operating position of HV0002 as Normally Closed

components.symbol_description: Underlined line number tag for 2" process pipe
components.position_hint: center, below the valve train, horizontal
components.notes: Full line number: 2"-PP01-567-GF0002-B03F9; PP=process pipe, spec B03F9

components.symbol_description: Vertical line number tag for 1" process pipe routed downward
components.notes: 1" drain or blowdown line; B03F9 pipe spec; -PP suffix may indicate process piping class; routed to grade

components.symbol_description: Pipe specification change label (two specs meeting)
components.notes: Indicates pipe spec change from B03F9 to B01M8 at this point on the drain line

components.symbol_description: Ground/grade level symbol — diagonal hatching with label and downward arrow with 'MIN.' dimension
components.notes: Drain line terminates at or near grade; MIN. arrow indicates minimum elevation or drop distance to grade

components.symbol_description: 16"x12" concentric or eccentric reducer symbol on nozzle N4 inlet
components.notes: Size marked '16"x12"'; line above is cut off at tile top — continues into r1c1/r1c2 tile

components.symbol_description: 16"x12" concentric or eccentric reducer symbol on nozzle N5 inlet
components.position_hint: upper right, above N5, adjacent to N4 reducer
components.notes: Identical to reducer above N4; both feed 12" nozzles from 16" headers

components.symbol_description: Hatched rectangular symbol with 'NOTE-2' label; appears twice on nozzles N4 and N5
components.position_hint: upper right, below reducers, on N4 and N5
components.notes: Likely references a drawing note specifying spectacle blind, slip blind, or special flange requirement

components.symbol_description: Small 'IG' tag/label appearing near valves — likely Inert Gas or Instrument Gas connection indicator
components.notes: IG appears in small font near valves; could denote Inert Gas purge, Instrument Gas, or be part of instrument tag; context unclear without legend

pipes.notes: 2" utility supply line entering from left to local panel/conditioner
Line reduces from 2" to 0.75" before HV0001, then returns to 2" — orifice/restriction arrangement
Short 2" spool between HV0001 and HV0051
Upper bridle leg of level gauge assembly
Lower bridle leg of level gauge assembly
1" drain line descending vertically from vessel; spec change B03F9→B01M8 partway down; terminates at grade level with MIN. distance indicated
Top of tile cut off; 16" inlet line feeds N4 via reducer
Top of tile cut off; 16" inlet line feeds N5 via reducer

edge_components: N17 — cut off at right edge; continues into r2c2
HV0026 — cut off at right edge; continues into r2c2
HV0027 — cut off at right edge; continues into r2c2
REDUCER-16x12-N4 / REDUCER-16x12-N5 — top connections cut off; continue into r1c1 or r1c2 tile above
16" header lines above N4 and N5 — cut off at top edge
N17 inlet line — cut off at top-right corner
IG and CO2 connections on right edge — partial; continue into r2c2
